def: v.t. To trade, traffic ( = banaj karnā); to dispose of.
s.m. Agreement, engagement; opportunity, occasion, opening

ety: ban = S. वन+dim. aff. ak = S. क
def: s.f. A sort of fruit; a kind of satin; pimple; drops of sweat on the face.
adv. In the name (of); to the address (of).
adj. Left (hand) = bāmā, bāṅwāṅ, bāyāṅ, q.q.v.

v.t. To make, form, fashion, shape, mould, create, prepare, get ready, manufacture, construct, compose, build, fabricate, invent, do, perform, finish, complete; to repair, mend, rectify, adjust, adorn, decorate, deck, trim; to colour, paint, polish; to cause to agree, make harmonize, to reconcile; to pluck (a fowl); to dress or cook (food); to put on, assume (a look, air, &c.), to feign, pretend; to mock; to hoax
subs: banā-lenā, v.t. intens. forms of, and = banānā (for banā-lānā, see s.v. past conj. part. banā).
wety: Urdu verb set بَنْنا (bannā) بَنانا (banānā) بَنْوانا (banvānā) Regular causative form of بننا (bannā).

def: v.n. See s.v. bannā.

s.m. Preparation, formation, composition, fabric, &c. ( = banāwaṭ, q.v.); dressing, preparing, decking (oneself); decoration, embellishment, adornment; good understanding, harmony, concord, mutual agreement (of persons, things, or qualities), reconciliation; adjustment, amendment, correction, remedy
subs: (-kā), To adorn, bedeck, ornament, decorate, embellish
(-se), A good understanding to exist (between or with), to be on good terms, get on well (together).

def: v.t. = banānā, q.v.
s.m. A Brāhmaṇ of the third order who has passed through the stages of student and householder and has left his house and family for the woods (syn. āsram); hermit, anchorite.
s.m. Monkey = bandar, q.v.
s.f. The holy city Benares.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀯𑀸𑀭𑀸𑀡𑀲𑀻 (vārāṇasī), from Sanskrit वाराणसी (vārāṇasī) (which is a metathesized form). Alternatively, named after the Rājā Bānār, who had rebuilt the city.

def: s.m. lit. 'King of the wood'; a large forest tree; a large tree bearing fruit, but apparently having no blossoms (as several species of the fig, the jack-tree, &c.); tree (generally).
s.f. = banāspattī, q.v.
s.f. Forest leaves; wild fruit; grass, herbage, vegetation, the vegetable kingdom.
adj. Having, or adorned with, gold or silver edging; — s.f. Lace; band or riband studded with spangles; gold or silver lace or edging worn on a cap or mantle.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic بِنْت (bint).

def: s.f. Broad-cloth.
s.f. Broad-cloth = banāt, q.v.

ety: of بنت bint, fem. of ibn or bin, rt. بني
def: s.f. The tender herbage
subs: s.m. The constellation of the Greater or Lesser Bear (consisting of seven stars, four of which are conceived to bear the resemblance of a naʻsh or bier, and the remaining three to be the banāt or daughters preceding it).

def: adj. Made of broad-cloth; woollen.
adj. Woollen; made of broad-cloth.
s.f. Fighting with cudgels; club-fight.
s.f. Getting ready, preparing; preparation; art, skill, dexterity.

s.f. Make, structure, construction, build, formation, composition, manufacture, workmanship, work; frame, form, fashion, figure, shape, mould, conformation, formation, appearance; fabrication, invention, contrivance, art, artifice, affectation; fiction, sham, pretence; forgery, counterfeit; show, display, embellishment, get up.
adj. Ninety-two.
part. (of banānā), Made, &c.
subs: v.n. To be making, be in hand (e.g. banāyā jātā hai, 'It is being made').

def: s.m. Eel.
s.f. Prating, jabber, senseless talk
subs: To prate, talk foolishly, &c.; to blurt out (a secret, &c.); to make a disagreeable noise, bellow, howl.

def: s.f. = baṅwar, q.v.
s.m. See s.v. بن for this and all compounds having ban for the first member.

ety: S. वल्मीक; — cf. S. वाम, and H. bām and bāṅb
def: s.f. Hillock or ground thrown up by white ants, ant-hill; snake's hole (since ant-hills deserted by ants are commonly taken possession of by snakes).

ety: S. वल्मीक; — cf. S. वाम, and H. bām and bāṅb
def: s.f. Hillock or ground thrown up by white ants, ant-hill; snake's hole (since ant-hills deserted by ants are commonly taken possession of by snakes).

ety: S. वल्मीक; — cf. S. वाम, and H. bām and bāṅb
def: s.f. Hillock or ground thrown up by white ants, ant-hill; snake's hole (since ant-hills deserted by ants are commonly taken possession of by snakes).
s.f. Reading, elocution (see next).
v.t. = baćānā, q.v.
v.t. To cause to read, to make, or hear (one) read ( = baṅćwānā).
s.f. Wish, desire.
part. adj. Wished for, desired, longed for.

ety: Prk. वांछणअं = S. वाञ्छनीय, rt. वाञ्छ्
def: v.n. To wish (for), to desire.

ety: bāṅć˚ = Prk. वच्च(इ), fr. S. वाच्य
def: v.t. To read; to study; to decipher, make out.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit वाचयति (vācayati, “causes to speak”). Compare Konkani वाजुचे (vājuce), वाच्चे (vācce), Gujarati વાંચવું (vā̃cvũ), Marathi वाचणे (vācṇe), Punjabi ਵਾਚਣਾ (vācṇā) / واچݨا (vācṇā).

def: v.n. To be read or perused.
v.t. To pass over, overlook, disregard, neglect; — also (prov.) = baćnā, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit वाचयति (vācayati, “causes to speak”). Compare Konkani वाजुचे (vājuce), वाच्चे (vācce), Gujarati વાંચવું (vā̃cvũ), Marathi वाचणे (vācṇe), Punjabi ਵਾਚਣਾ (vācṇā) / واچݨا (vācṇā).

def: v.n. To cause to be read, to have (a thing) read; (caus. of bāṅćnā), to cause to read, to make, or to hear (one) read.

ety: Pehl. band, Zend banda, S. बन्ध bandha
def: s.m. Binding, fastening, tie, knot, band, bandage, bond, ligature, belt, roll, string; joint, knuckle; stoppage, discontinuance; hindrance, prevention, obstacle; fetters, imprisonment, arrest, captivity, confinement; a mound, dyke, dam, embankment; gripe, hold, grasp, twist, trick (in wrestling); manœuvre, dodge, artifice; art, skill, dexterity; agreement, promise, pledge, contract; regulation; list, inventory; verse, stanza, burden (of a song); — (as an affix) binding; binder, fastener, putter on (e.g. naʻl-band, one who puts on horse-shoes, a farrier)
subs: v.n. To construct a dam, dyke, embankment, &c.
s.f. Account of each share of assessment in grain
s.f. Account of the share of an instalment to be paid by each villager
s.m. Every joint; every part, every inch
band band judā karnā or kar-denā
(-kā), To sever all the joints, open all the knobs or fastenings, separate inch by inch
s.f. Statement of the amount of each money instalment of a village
v.n. lit. 'The joints to fit in'; to succeed in an affair
s.m. Account of the shares of the liabilities of a village
s.m. Damming up a watercourse for the purpose of irrigation
s.m. The act of allotting to each cultivator an equal portion of good and bad lands
s.m. Stated period for the settlement of an account
band-meṅ paṛnā, v.n. To fall into the toils; to become a captive

s.m. lit. 'Binding and fastening'; plan, organization, management, administration; arrangement, disposition, method, order, system; settlement (of revenue), regulation; — band-o-bast-i-ākhir, s.m. Recent or last settlement; — band-o-bast-i istimrārī, s.m. Permanent settlement; settlement in perpetuity; — band-o-bast-i ćand-roza, s.m. Temporary settlement; — band-o-bast-i-jadīd, band-o-bast-i ḥāl, s.m. New settlement; new regulation or practice
s.m. Permanent settlement ( = band-o-bast-i istimrārī); — band-o-bast-i-sar-sarī, s.m. = band-o-bast-i ćand-roza; — band-o-bast-i sābiq, s.m. Former settlement
(-kā), To manage, regulate, settle, make an arrangement, arrange (for), organize ( = intiz̤ām karnā, q.v.)
s.m. Sum total of the revenue of a province or kingdom
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَنْد (band).

ety: part. of bastan; Pehl. band, Z. banda; S. बद्ध, rt. बन्ध्
def: part. adj. Fastened, tied up, bound; shut, closed, stopped, stopped up, cut off; prevented, hindered, barred, checked, restrained, suppressed; constrained, still, dull, heavy, paralyzed; contracted, straitened
subs: v.n. To be engaged in a business
v.t. To shut, close, shut up, confine, imprison; to stop, stop up, fill up (a hole, &c.); to arrest, check, put a stop to, hinder, mar; to settle, wind up (an account)
or ho-jānā, v.n. To be closed, stopped, cut off, shut up, stopped up; to be finished, be exhausted, to cease; to be abolished, be done away, be lost or gone.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَنْد (band).

def: s.m. Name of a place in Bundelkhand.
s.m. The parasitical plant Epidendrum tesselatum; a parasitical plant (syn. ākāś-vela).
adj. & s.m. Maimed, crippled; docked; tailless; — a tailless bird; a tailless snake; a man who has been circumcised (applied as a term of abuse to Musalmans, especially in Bengal).
s.m. Mistletoe, Epidendron teselloides ( = bāṅdā, q.v.).
s.m. A grain magazine above ground (cf. bādār).
s.m. = P. بنده banda, q.v.
s.m. Mistletoe, &c. ( = bandā, q.v.).
s.m. Slave, bondman, servant; humble servant (a term used by a speaker of himself, when addressing a superior); human being, creature
subs: s.m. Cherisher of servants or dependants, patron; your honour, your worship
s.m. Your slave's son, my son

def: s.m. pl. (of banda), Slaves, servants.
s.f. Slavery, servitude; service; devotion, adoration, worship, praise; compliment, salutation; humility, lowliness; — intj. My service to you! good morning! good-bye! adieu! farewell! thank you!
subs: v.n. To affect authority while in servitude.

def: s.m. pl. (of banda), Slaves, servants.
s.m. Fastening, &c. ( = bandhan, q.v.)
s.m. Praise, worship, adoration; obeisance, homage, salutation; flattery
subs: (S. vandana+mālā), s.f. A festoon of leaves and flowers suspended across gateways on festive occasions.

def: s.f. See s.v. bandan. (The word is sometimes incorrectly written bandhan-wār, as though derived from bandhana and dwāra.)
s.m. Monkey, ape
subs: (S. vānara+kshata), s.m. A sore that does not heal (as a monkey-bite); a running sore, an issue.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بندر (bandar). Compare Bengali বন্দর (bondor), Dhivehi ބަނދަރު (baⁿdaru).

def: s.m. Inlet (of the sea), harbour, anchorage, sea-port, emporium, city, trading town (on the sea) to which numbers of foreign merchants resort.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بندر (bandar). Compare Bengali বন্দর (bondor), Dhivehi ބަނދަރު (baⁿdaru).

def: s.m. = bandar, q.v.
s.m. Brink (of a river), shore, coast; skirt, side (of a garden. — The word is peculiar to Southern India, and would appear to be connected with bandar).
s.f. A sort of chintz from Maćhlī-bandar; a sword from Rāj-bandarī.
s.f. A female monkey; a kind of grass found in rice fields and in kodoṅ, and used as fodder.
s.f. A female monkey ( = bandrī).
s.m. See s.v. bandar, 'monkey.'
s.f. (of bāṅdar) = bandarī, q.v.
s.f. Ridge-pole; ridge of a house.
part. adj. Bound, fastened, &c. ( = band)
subs: s.m. A complete army (with its four divisions of elephants, chariots, horse, and foot; — syn. ćatur-anga)
bandh-meṅ paṛnā, v.n. To fall into the toils or meshes, to become a captive.

def: s.m. Fastening; tie, fetter; confinement, imprisonment; embankment; dam.
part. adj. (f. -ī), Fastened, tied, bound, fixed, established, continuous, uninterrupted, usual, ordinary; shut up, concealed, undisclosed
subs: s.f. Monotonous tone, monotony or sameness in singing
s.f. Established thing, settled matter, fixed rule
s.f. Undisclosed intention, secret; compact and united family
adj. = bandhā; — bandhā-huʼā ḵẖarć, s.m. Regular or usual expenses.

def: s.m. = bāndhav, q.v.
adj. Fastened, bound, tied.
s.f. The act of binding, tying, &c.; the business of binding; binding, fastening, tying; bond, tie; price paid for binding; premium paid in exchange of money of inferior denomination.
s.m. Binding; confinement; barter; pawn, pledge, deposit, mortgage ( = rahn); mortgage-bond ( = rahn-nāma); hostage; wages.
s.f. Unchaste woman, harlot; barren woman.
s.m. Name of a tribe inhabiting a part of Bundelkhand.
s.m. Fixed allowance, wages, salary, stipend, pension; raised embankments of earth for flooding lands; purchase of grain in advance of harvest.

s.m. Fastening, binding, tying; tie, knot; bandage; bond, confinement, imprisonment, captivity, snare, toils; obstacle, hindrance, check, restraint; established rule or custom, daily practice or observance
v.t. To cause to be bound, to have or get bound; to bind, tie, fasten, &c.; to enjoin upon; to fix, establish.
s.f. One who carries stones, timber, &c. on his shoulder, porter, cooly; pensioner, stipendiary; gunner, artilleryman.
s.f. Instrument for tying or holding together, tie, string, rope, thread, chain, snare; wrapper, &c. ( = bandhnā).
s.m. Relation, relative, kinsman, friend.
s.m. = bandhu.
s.f. Binding, fastening; tie, &c. (see bāṅdhnā and bandhāʼī).
s.m. Charm against miscarriage
subs: s.f. Astringent medicine; remedy for diarrhœa.

def: s.m. Charm against miscarriage
subs: s.f. Astringent medicine; remedy for diarrhœa.

def: s.f. Embankment, &c. = bandhān, and band, qq.v.
s.f. Embankment, &c. = bandhān, and band, qq.v.

ety: bāṅdh˚ = Prk. बंध(इ) = S. बध्ना(ति), rt. बन्ध्
def: v.t. To bind, tie, fix, fasten; to tie up, tighten; to bind up, dress (hair, &c.); to fasten or tie (on or round), bind (round), put (on, -par; e.g. ghāʼo-par pulṭis bāṅdho); to chain, enchain, fetter; to take captive, enthral, captivate, bring under (one's) influence; to shut up, confine, imprison; to fix, direct, fasten (hope, eyes, aim, &c. upon, -par); to shut, close, stop (water, &c.), to embank; to arrest, hold back, check; to bind together, join together (in marriage, &c.), fasten together, put together, join, connect, conglomerate, unite, gather, pack, set (e.g. gathrī bāṅdhnā; haḍḍī bāṅdhnā; hāth bāṅdhnā; laḍḍū bāṅdhnā; maẓmūn bāṅdhnā); to build, construct (dam, bridge, &c.); to compose (verses); to form, produce, make, constitute (e.g. ṣaf bāṅdhnā; nīyat bāṅdhnā; samā bāṅdhnā); to entertain, cherish, foster, have, contract, form (enmity, friendship, &c.); to put on, lay on, impose, levy (e.g. maḥsūl bāṅdhnā); to put on, lay (a stake, wager, &c.); to fix, erect, set up (a post, standard, &c.); to devise, contrive, plan; to make (of, -ko), constitute, regard as, compare to (e.g. tū usko tār bāṅdh, 'you regard her as, or liken her to, a palm')
subs: v.t. (intens. of bāṅdhnā), To bind up, tie up, &c.

def: v.n. To be fastened, bound, tied, kept; to be enclosed, be closed or stopped, be laid; to be caught, be ensnared; to be formed or composed; to be continuous or uninterrupted, be fixed or established; to be conducive, to help, co-operate; — s.m. Instrument for tying, tie, rope, &c. ( = bandhnī, q.v.); cloth for wrapping small articles in, wrapper; needle-case; hold-all
subs: v.n. To be formed, be composed, &c. = bandhnā.

def: s.m. Invention, fabrication; slander, calumny; plan, plot; story, fiction, romance; a mode of dying in which the cloth is tied up in different places to prevent the parts tied from receiving the dye; a checked or spotted cloth; a kind of coloured silk (syn. gul-badan); a species of parrot
s.m. See بندهو bandhu.

s.m. Kinsman, relation, relative, connection, kindred in general; friend; (in law) cognate kinsman in a remote degree (one subsequent in right of inheritance to the sa-gotra); one of the same brotherhood, or tribe, or profession; — bandhu, bandhu-jīva, s.m. The flower and plant Pentapetes Phœnicea ( = bandhur)
subs: s.m. lit. 'Given by relations'; a particular kind of female property (given to a girl by her relations at her marriage)
adj. Destitute of kindred, relationless, friendless.

def: s.m. The shrub Pentapetes Phænicea ( = bandhur); the plant Ixora bandhu
subs: or bandhūk, s.m. The tree Terminalia tomentosa.

def: s.m. A shrub bearing a red flower, Pentapetes Phœnicea, and its flower; name of a drug (syn. birang rishabh); a crane.
s.m. Purchase of grain in ad. vance of harvest.
s.f. Harlot, prostitute.
s.f. Friendship; relationship, affinity, connection, relation; relations collectively, kindred, kin.
s.m. One who is bound, prisoner, convict, bondman, captive, slave.
s.f. Imprisonment, bondage, captivity.
s.f. Relationship, kinship, friendship.
s.f. = bandhāʼī, q.v.
v.t. To cause to be bound, to have or get (a person or a thing) bound, tied, &c.; to cause to be constructed, framed, prepared, &c.
s.f. Land embanked all round in such a manner as to retain water.
adj. & s.f. (m. bandhya), Barren, sterile; — barren woman; childless woman; barren cow (or other animal); — a perfume (commonly called bālā).
adj. Bound, confined, detained under arrest; barren, sterile, unfruitful.
s.f. A female slave, bondmaid (syn. launḍī)
subs: bāṅdī-kā beṭā or janā, s.m. lit. 'Son of a bondmaid'; an obedient or submissive person.

def: s.m. Prisoner, captive, slave; — one who praises or extols, praiser, panegyrist, bard (these bards are regarded as belonging to a distinct tribe called Bhāṭ)
subs: s.m. A bard, a person of the Bhāṭ tribe
s.m. The substance of a Bhāṭ's teaching

def: s.f. A kind of ornament worn on the head; — a short robe or dress (shorter than the jāma; cf. banḍī; — name of a certain dry measure
s.f. A short robe ( = bandī, q.v.); waistcoat, vest.
s.f. Bullock cart or car, a bandy (the word is peculiar to Southern India).

s.f. Binding, tying, fastening; shutting, closing, stopping; construction, &c.; (in Law and Revenue) arrangement, settlement, agreement; distribution, allowance; reduction; charge (used in comp. e.g. naʻl-bandī; jamʻ-bandī; qq.v.); — imprisonment, captivity; — s.m.f. Prisoner, captive, slave; female slave or servant, handmaid (cf. banda)
subs: s.m. House of bondage, prison, gaol ( = bandī-ghar).

def: s.f. A short thick stick, cudgel, club; a kind of dress
subs: s.m. Cudgeller; clubman, one who carries and uses a club
bāṅḍī ćalnā, v.n. Clubs to be set a-going; a fight with clubs taking place.

def: s.f. The act, or state, of tying, binding, &c.; make, structure, build, &c. ( = banāwaṭ); construction, composition; knot, entanglement; invention, contrivance, plan, scheme, artifice, manœuvre; fabrication, preparing a false story, making up a false account of a trausaction; combination, league, plot, conspiracy, collusion, cabal, intrigue; elegance (of style)
subs: v.n. To form a league or conspiracy, to contrive, plot together, cabal, conspire.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَنْدِش (bandiš).

def: s.m. Prisoner, captive.
s.f. A species of rice (grown in Rohelkhanḍ).
v.t. = bāṅdhnā, q.v.
v.n. = bandhnā, q.v.
s.m. Whirlwind ( = bandūhā).
s.m. See s.v. band.
wety: From Classical Persian بَنْد (band) + و (u) + بَسْت (bast).

def: s.m. Whirlwind ( = bandūhā).
s.m. Child of a slave ( = baṅdor, q.v.).
s.f. Slave-girl, female slave; child of a slave.
s.f. Slave-girl, female slave; child of a slave.
s.m. Mistletoe ( = bandā, q.v.).
s.m. = bandar, q.v.
s.f. (of bāṅdar) = bandarī, q.v.
s.m. = bandhuʼā, q.v.
s.m. Whirlwind.

ety: from بندق bunduq, 'bullet,' 'ball,' the Arabicized form of the Persian funduq
def: s.f. Musket, firelock, gun, fowling piece
subs: v.n. To take aim with a gun, to point a gun (at)
(par), bandūq ćhoṛnā, bandūq lagānā, bandūq mārnā (-ko), To discharge a gun, to fire (at); to shoot.
wety: From Classical Persian بَنْدُوق (bandūq), from Arabic بُنْدُق (bunduq, “hazelnut, pebble, bullet, gun”), from Middle Persian pndk' (pondik, “hazelnut”), from Ancient Greek Ποντικόν κάρυον (Pontikón káruon, “Pontic nut”).

def: s.m. Musketeer, rifleman.
adj. Pertaining to the forest; sylvan, wild, savage.
s.f. A torch lighted at both ends and whirled round so as toform a double circle of fire; a club weighted at both ends which is whirled round the head as a gymnastic exercise (cf. banauṭ)
subs: v.n. To whirl or twirl a banaiṭhī; to flourish a cudgel; to strike out right and left with a stick.

def: s.f. A torch lighted at both ends and whirled round so as toform a double circle of fire; a club weighted at both ends which is whirled round the head as a gymnastic exercise (cf. banauṭ)
subs: v.n. To whirl or twirl a banaiṭhī; to flourish a cudgel; to strike out right and left with a stick.

ety: rt. Zend vać; S. वाच्, cf. āwāz
def: s.f. Voice, sound, noise, cry, shout; the call to prayer by the muʼaẕẕin from the minarets or towers of the mosques (syn. aẕān); crowing (of a cock)
subs: v.n. To call Musalmāns to prayer; to crow (a cock)
s.f. The morning call to prayer
v.n. To cry out; to call
s.f. The call to prayer ( = bāṅg).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بانْگ.

def: s.f. Tin, lead; calx of tin (taken internally as an aphrodisiac); — the egg-plant, Solanum melongena; — name of the province of Bengal proper (Bangālā)
subs: (S. vaṅga+kaṇṭa+ika), s.m. The prickly plant Solanum Jacquini, resembling a thistle (used medicinally for diarrhœa and coughs; syn. ban-kaṭaiyā; bhaṭ-kaṭaiyā).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَنْگ (bang). Doublet of بھان٘گ (bhāṅg).

def: s.f. Hemp, Cannabis satica; an intoxicating potion made from the leaves of hemp ( = bhāṅg).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَنْگ (bang). Doublet of بھان٘گ (bhāṅg).

def: s.m. Cotton with the seeds in it, undressed cotton; the cotton plant (syn. kapās: — the word is almost peculiar to Bengal proper).
s.m. Joint of bamboo root; piece of bamboo.
s.m. Well-water having a slightly metallic or brackish taste; a kind of soil.
= بانگا bāṅgā, q.v.
= next, q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَنْگَال (bangāl), borrowed from Middle Bengali বাঙ্গালা (baṅgala), from Sanskrit वङ्गाल (vaṅgāla), from वङ्ग (vaṅga).

def: s.m. Name of the province of Bengal.
s.f. A woman of the Bangālī race, a female Bengalee.
adj. & s.m. Relating, or pertaining, to Bengal; of Bengal, Bengalee; — native of Bengal; — s.f. The Bengalee language.
s.f. A woman of the Bangālī race, a female Bengalee.
s.f. The Bengalee language; — a small bungalow; cottage; — a species of rice.
s.m. Hilly ground, high ground, upland, highland, tableland (cf. ḍāṅg).
s.m. An instrument or meter for ascertaining the strength of liquors.
s.f. Model, specimen, sample, example, pattern, muster.
s.m. One who is addicted to taking bang; one who intoxicates himself with bang.
s.f. Humming top (syn. bhauṅrā).
s.m. An aquatic beetle which eats rice plants.
s.m. See s.v. bang (also written ban-kaṭaiyā).
s.f. A species of rice cultivated in Benares (originally, perhaps, from Bengal).

ety: contrac. from bangālā; or from S. baṅga, or vaṅga
def: s.f. The Bengalee language; — s.m. A thatched house, a 'bungalow'; summer-house; cottage; — a sort of betel-leaf or pān.
s.f. A bracelet or 'bangle' made of glass or lac (also written baṅglī, vaṅglī; — ćūrī).
s.f. A bracelet or 'bangle' made of glass or lac (also written baṅglī, vaṅglī; — ćūrī).
adj. & s.m. Of or pertaining to the bāṅgar or 'hills'; a native of the highlands, &c.; — rude, rough, uncivilized, boorish; ignorant, stupid.

s.f. The arm (from the elbow to the shoulder); sleeve; support, help; supporter, protector; security, guarantee (as when a person trusts himself in the power of an enemy, a third person, who engages to return him to his home, &c. in security, is his bāṅh); power, strength
subs: lit. 'To have the arm raised'; to be in a position of power, &c.; to be powerful
s.m. Strength of arm; support, ally; a younger brother; — bāṅh-balī, adj. & s.m. Strong in the arm; — one who is strong in the arm
bāṅh gahnā (-kī), lit. 'To hold the arm (of)'; to aid, protect, support
(-kī), To lose one's prop, support, protector, &c.; to be destitute of friends or protectors
(-par), To tuck up the sleeves (for, or against); to get ready (for)
(-ko), To lend an arm (to), to support, assist, aid.

def: s.m. Conjurer, magician, wizard.
s.f. Wife of a conjurer; female conjurer, enchantress.
s.m. Support, ally, protector, patron.
s.m. Bridegroom ( = banā; banrā).

s.f. Peculiar disposition or mental constitution; natural temper; profession, pretension; any property or virtue, &c. upon which one prides or plumes oneself; fabric, structure; price paid for making a thing or for doing a work (syn. banwāʼī); — a yellow earth with which potters ornament vessels; — ashes; — a measure of weight equal to eighty rupees
subs: (apnī), To take one's stand upon one's pretension, dignity, &c.; to be roused so as to let one's temper appear, to show one's natural wrath, &c.

def: s.f. Weaving; thread with which cloth is woven
subs: s.f. Price or cost of weaving.

def: s.f. Sound, note, voice; speech, word, language, discourse; precept, doctrine; sectarian verses of mendicants; name of the goddess of speech (Sarasvatī); — adj. (used in comp.), voiced, tongued, &c. (e.g. amr̤it-bānī).

s.m. Builder, architect, framer, founder, author, composer, inventor, contriver; source, origin; beginner, instigator; bānī-kār, s.m. Builder, &c. = bānī; — adj. & s.m. Skilful, clever, expert (in, -meṅ), astute; — consummate rogue, clever rascal; swindler, sharper
subs: s.f. Building, architecture; composition; commencing a business; instigation; astuteness; rascality.

def: s.f. Small forest, wood, grove.
s.f. Bride.

ety: obl. pl. of ibn, rt. بني 'to build'
def: s.m. Sons of Adam, men, mortals
subs: s.m. The children of Jān, the Jinn or Genii, the beings who inhabited the world before Adam.

def: s.m. Ploughman or labourer whose services are paid in bannī or in kind.
s.m. Trader, merchant ( = baṇik); the sixth of the astronomical periods called karaṇ, corresponding to the half of a lunar day.
s.m. = banij, q.v.

ety: S. बणिज्; or बणिज्य for बाणिज
def: s.m. Trade, traffic, commerce, mercantile transactions, merchandise
s.f. The business of a pedlar.
s.m. Trade, commerce, merchandise, traffic ( = banij, q.v.).
s.m. Trade, &c. = banij, q.v.
s.m. Merchant, trader (cf. baniyā)
subs: s.m. Merchant's son, son of a trader; young merchant or trader
s.f. Merchant's daughter; a young woman of the merchant (banyā) class; — baṇik-path, s.m. lit. 'Traders' path or line'; traffic, commerce.

def: s.m. = banik, q.v.
prep. In respect (of, -ke), with respect (to), with relation (to); in comparison (with)
s.f. Woman; beloved woman, wife, mistress.
s.m. Name of a subdivision of the Bhangī caste.
s.m. Merchant, trader, shopkeeper, corn-chandler, grain-seller, vender of provisions (syn. baqqāl); — (met.) petty-minded man; niggard; timid man.
s.m. = baniyā, q.v.
s.f. Wife of a banyā; woman of the baniyā caste.
s.f. Wife of a banyā; woman of the baniyā caste.
adj. Barren, &c. = bānjh, q.v.
s.f. Henbane.
adj. Waste or fallow (land); — see banjar.
s.m. Waste land unfit for cultivation; unproductive land, fallow land; waste, wild, jungle
subs: s.m. Land brought recently into cultivation after lying some time fallow
s.m. Waste land excluded from the rental
s.m. Land left fallow for some years
s.f. Abatement of rent in consideration of waste land.

def: s.m. A caste or class who are carriers of grain, salt, &c.; an individual of that class; a grain merchant.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀡𑀺𑀚𑁆𑀚𑀸𑀭 (vaṇijjāra, “trader”), from Sanskrit वणिज्या (vaṇijyā́, “trade”) + कार (kāra, “doer”).

def: adj. Belonging to, or relating to, the Banjārās.
s.f. Wife of a banjārā; a kind of tent used by banjārās; half-boiled grain.
adj. Barren (woman); unproductive, sterile (soil).
s.m. The tree Quercus incana, or dilatata (common in the outer Himalaya, the acorns of which are commonly made into necklaces for children, and are regarded as a charm against evil spirits).
Pill or bolus given to destroy the embryo; medicine taken to produce barrenness.
s.m. (?), Land close by a village
subs: (about three feet high) which springs up with the ḵẖarīf crops.

def: s.m. Crookedness, curvature; crook, curve, bend, winding, sweep; bend or reach of a river, winding course of a stream; craftiness, duplicity; wickedness, offence, fault; — a hook or curved instrument to cut bamboos and sugar-cane with ( = bāṅkā); a dagger with a curved blade (in this and the following senses the word is usually fem.); a semicircular armlet (syn. bāzū); a kind of anklet; a settee; exercise with the dagger
subs: s.m. A method of fencing with a (wooden) dagger and cutlass.

def: s.m. Bank
s.m. Bending, curvature; bend, elbow, or reach of a river or winding stream.

adj. & s.m. Crooked, winding, curved, bent, awry, on one side; oblique, tortuous, retrograde (motion of planets); crooked in disposition, cunning, fraudulent; malevolent, refractory, rebellious; — foppish, coxcombish, dashing, showy, smart, gay, coquettish, wanton; fine; spirited; — a fop, beau, buck, dandy; bravo, bully; dissolute fellow, rake, roué (syn. albelā); — a hook or curved instrument to cut bamboos, &c. with ( = bāṅk); a particular musical instrument, a bent trumpet made of brass
s.f. Bend, &c. = baṅk, q.v.
s.m. Beau, fop, &c. = bāṅkā.
s.f. Foppishness, &c. = bāṅkpan, q.v.
s.m. See s.v. ban, 'forest.'

part. (of bannā), Having become; — conj. & adv. As, like (often used as synonymous with hokar, when it either has the sense of 'as,' or gives to the preceding word the force of an adverb; e.g. mitra bankar mile jule raho, Live together as friends in peace and harmony; āp nir-daʼī ban taṭṭū-par ćalā jātā hai, He himself is going heartlessly along upon the pony).
v.n. To cry out, shout, yell, bellow, roar, thunder; to make answer (as one possessed by an evil spirit).
s.m. = baṅg-katiyā, q.v.s.v. bang.
part. (of bannā) = bankar, q.v.
s.m. See s.v. ban.
s.m. Crookedness, curvature, &c.; foppishness, coxcombry, dandyism, smartness, gayness, elegance; skittishness, wantonness; disorderly conduct, rakishness, debauchery.
s.m. A ruined or spoiled crop (on the field).
v.n. See بننا bannā.
ex: انسان پانی کے بنا جی نہیں سکتے
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀺𑀡𑀸 (viṇā), from Sanskrit विना (vínā). Doublet of وِنَا (vinā), a tatsama. Cognate with Punjabi ਬਿਨ (bin) / بِن (bin), Mewari वना (vanā) Kachchi વિણ- (viṇa-), Marathi बिन- (bin-).

def: s.m. Mason, builder.
ex: انسان پانی کے بنا جی نہیں سکتے
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀺𑀡𑀸 (viṇā), from Sanskrit विना (vínā). Doublet of وِنَا (vinā), a tatsama. Cognate with Punjabi ਬਿਨ (bin) / بِن (bin), Mewari वना (vanā) Kachchi વિણ- (viṇa-), Marathi बिन- (bin-).

ety: ban˚ = Prk. वण(इ) = S. वन्य(ते), pass. of rt. वन्
def: v.n. To be made, constructed, built; to be created, formed, fashioned, produced, fabricated, invented; to be prepared, got ready, be done, finished or completed; to be cooked or dressed; to be managed, executed, effected; to be composed; to be mended, repaired, or adjusted: to be improved, be made presentable, palatable, &c.; to be established, be set up; to be, become; to happen, befall, betide; to be as, become as or like (see ban-kar); to be adorned, decorated, embellished; to fit, fit in, come out, come right; to chime, agree, fall in (with), do, answer, serve; to succeed, do well, prosper; to be made, acquired, or gained; to be assumed, be feigned, to counterfeit; to be hoaxed, be ridiculed or laughed at
subs: ban-parnā, v.n. To be performed, effected, or accomplished; to do; to succeed, turn out well, to answer, suit; to be fortunate, to prosper; to come to pass
v.n. Intens. of, and = bannā, q.v.
v.n. To be decked out, be thoroughly got up, or elaborately dressed and adorned.

def: s.m. Name of a tribe of Rājpūts.
s.f. Portion of grain given to a labourer as a remuneration for his services.
s.f. Lady, &c. = bānū, q.v.; — A proper name.
s.m. See s.v. ban, 'forest.'
s.m. Bridegroom ( = bana, bannā, q.v.).
s.f. Bride ( = banī).
s.m. The bamboo, Bambusa arundinacea and stricta; a bamboo or rod, about ten feet long, used in measuring land, &c.
subs: bāṅs-par ćaṛhānā, v.t. To stick or raise up on a bamboo; (fig.) to hold up to ridicule, infamy, &c., to disgrace
v.n. To climb up a bamboo; to be stuck up on a bamboo; to be held up to ridicule, infamy, &c., to be branded with infamy
(S. vaṅśa+sphoṭa), s.m. lit. 'Bamboo-splitter'; a caste who engage in bamboo-work, manufacturing baskets, &c. out of split bamboos (syn. basoṛ, ćohāṛ, ḍom)
bāṅsoṅ kūdnā, v.n. To leap or bound for joy, to rejoice.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀁𑀲 (vaṃsa), from Sanskrit वंश (vaṃśa), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *wanćás.

def: s.m. Bamboo (used in comp. for bāṅs, q.v.); line of a pedigree or genealogy (from its resemblance to the succession of joints in a bamboo), lineage, race, family, stock; descendant, offspring
subs: (S. vaṅśa+āvalī), s.f. Line of a family, pedigree, genealogy, genealogical tree (syn. shajra)
(S. vaṅśa+sphoṭaka), s.m. lit. 'Bamboo-splitter'; one who makes baskets, chairs, &c. of split bamboos or reeds ( = basoṛ, q.v.)
(S. vaṅśa+roćanā or loćanā), s.m. An earthy concretion of a milk-white colour found in the hollow of the bamboo and known by the name of bamboo-manna (syn. tabāshīr)
s.f. Bamboo plantation; jungle of bamboos.

def: s.m. Bridge of the nose; back-bone, spine (syn. rīṛh); the bamboo tube through which the seed descends in a drilling machine; a drill-plough; — a plant of the bamboo species (a variety of the Dendrocalamus Tulda or pekā-bāṅs) from which a red dye is extracted (syn. piyā-bāṅsā).
s.m. A species of grass which grows in rice fields (it is used as fodder for cattle).
s.f. A weed (found in the Doāb), which is very injurious, and most difficult to eradicate.
s.f. See s.v. baṅs.
adj. of or pertaining to bamboo; made of bamboo.
s.f. Flute, pipe, &c. = bāṅsrī, q.v.; — reed of which ḥuqqa snakes are made; reed used by weavers and makers of artificial flowers; — a kind of stone.
s.f. Flute, pipe; a kind of wheat with blackish ears
subs: s.m. The tree under which Kr̤ishṇa was accustomed to play the flute.

def: adj. Pertaining to a family, race, &c.; lineal, genealogical; of the race or lineage (of).
s.f. Fishing-hook; fishing-line or tackle.
s.f. Flute, &c. = bāṅsrī, q.v.; — a purse ( = bāṅsnī).
s.f. A purse ( = basnī, q.v.).
s.f. Flute, &c. = bāṅsrī, baṅsī qq.v.
s.f. Pipe, fife, flute, flageolet
subs: v.n. To pipe, to play on the flute.

def: s.f. Bamboo thicket; bamboo plantation ( = baṅswāṛī).
s.f. See s.v. baṅs.
s.m. = bāṭ, 'a weight,' &c. q.v.
s.m.f. Dividing, apportioning; division, distribution (syn. taqsīm); part, portion, share, allotment; dealing, deal (at cards); lot, concern, business; — food given to a cow while milking her
subs: s.m. Apportioner, distributer = bāṅṭū; (in Arith.) denominator (syn. nasab-numā)
s.m. Deed or record of division of property (syn. taqsīm-nāma)
s.m. (in Arith.), Quotient (syn. ḵẖārij-qismat, and labdhi)

def: s.m. Share, part, division, &c. ( = baṭ; bāṅṭ; qq.v.).
s.m. Dividing, &c.; share, portion, &c. = bāṅṭ, q.v.; — tying up the crops into bundles and sheaves.
adj. Divided, apportioned, &c.
subs: adj. = bāṅṭā; — s.m. (in Arith.), Dividend (also baṭā-huʼā, syn. bhājya, and maqsūm).

def: s.m. A large brass water-pot.
s.m. Allotter, distributer, divider, &c. = baṭait, baṭwaiyā, qq.v
v.t. To cause to be shared; to distribute, share, divide, &c. = baṭānā, baṭwānā, qq.v.
adv. Adorned, embellished, elaborately decorated or dressed up.
adv. Adorned, embellished, elaborately decorated or dressed up.
v.t. = bāṅṭnā, 'to divide,' &c.
v.n. To be twisted, &c. = baṭnā, q.v.
v.t. To divide, apportion, distribute, portion out, parcel out, divide into shares; to dispense, dispose of, assign; to participate, go shares in
subs: v.t. To divide, portion out (for, or in the name of, -par), &c. = bāṅṭnā, q.v.
v.t. To participate, go shares in, &c.

def: v.n. To be shared, &c. = baṭnā, q.v.
v.t. To twist, twine.
s.m. Divider, apportioner, &c.; (in Arith.), divisor (syn. bhājak, and maqsūm-ilaih).
s.m. One who allots shares, one who distributes; divider, distributer ( = baṭwaiyā, q.v.).
v.t. = baṭwānā, baṭānā, qq.v.
s.m. = baṭwārā, q.v.
s.f. Lady; woman of rank, gentlewoman.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بانو (bānū, “lady; queen”).

def: s.m. A kind of water-bird.
s.m. = H بانوسي बानूसी bānūsī, s.f. A kind of cloth.
s.f. = H بانوسا बानूसा bānūsā, s.m. A kind of cloth.
s.f. Cost of making (a thing), price paid for inaking, &c.

ety: banwā-, rt. of banwānā+iyā = S. ikah
def: s.m. Maker, manufacturer.
adj. & s.m. Left; left hand, left side ( = bāyāṅ, q.v.).
v.t. To cause to be made, constructed, or prepared, &c.; to have or get (a thing) made, &c. (see bannā); to make, construct, &c. ( = banānā).
s.m.f = baṅwar, q.v.
s.m. Creeper, &c. = baṅwar, q.v.
s.m.f. Creeper; vine; tendril (also written baṅwaṅḍ, baṅwaṅr).
s.m.f. Creeper; vine; tendril (also written baṅwaṅḍ, baṅwaṅr).
adj. = ban-mālī, q.v.s.v. ban.
adj. Left (hand) = bāyāṅ, bāṅwāṅ, q.q.v.
see s.v. bāʼo.
adj. (f. -ī), Mad, crazy, deranged, demented, insane.

ety: S. वापि+dim. aff. lī = S. र
def: s.f. A large masonry well, generally with winding steps down to the water, and landing-places and chambers in the surrounding wall.
s.f. The drag (thing or animal), with which hawks, dogs, &c. are trained; lure; effigy; delusion, trick (syn. dhokā)
adj. & s.m. Dwarfish; a dwarf, &c. = bāman, q.v.
s.f. Seed-time; sowing.
s.f. Community, company, assembly, society; company of persons who raise a fund for a nāć in the Holī festival.
adj. = bāʼolā, q.v.
s.f. Name of a tribe of vagrants to the west of Dehli.
s.f. = bāʼolī, 'a large well,' q.v.
s.f. = bāʼolī, 'a large well,' q.v.
s.m. Flag (see next).
s.m. Flag. banner, standard; — rheumatism; cramp; spasms; convulsions — bāʼoṭā uṛānā, v.n. To hoist a flag; — to make mince-meat of, to crush.
s.m. A kind of armlet (of gold or silver) worn by Hindū women.
s.m. Father; senior, elder, superior
subs: intj. A cry for help, quarter, or mercy
bāp karnā, v.t. To consider or regard as a father
To go the length of abusing one's father
bāp-re-bāp, bāp-merā, intj. Exclamations of surprise, grief, &c.
(-ke), To be regarded as an inheritance from (one's) father, to be kept as one's own property (applied in the case of property, &c. which is borrowed and never returned)
s.m. Blood-feud caused by the murder of a father; family-feud

def: adj. On foot; standing, raised, established
adj. & s.f. = bapautī, q.v.
s.m. Father's portion, patrimony, inheritance, &c. ( = bapotī, q.v.).
adj. Secret, hidden, concealed.
adj. & s.f. Inherited from a father, patrimonial, hereditary, ancestral; — patrimony, inheritance, heritage, paternal estate, ancestral property
subs: s.m. Hereditary right; right by or of succession
s.m. Patrimony, inherited property; ancestral property.

def: s.f. Steam, vapour, exhalation ( = bhāp).
s.m. Vapour, &c. = bhapārā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = bapautī, q.v.
s.f. Large oblong reservoir; pond, pool.
s.f. = bāʼolī, q.v.
adj. Poor, helpless, destitute.
